title: Obstacle-triggered safety stop
_about: Reusable incident rule. A compiler (tools/incident_spec.py, not built yet) evaluates this against an incident's timeline/logs and EMITS the annotations shape that tools/incident_rules.py already consumes. Signals declare HOW to find/observe evidence in the data; anchor times (t) are discovered by the compiler via anchor_strategy, never written here. The deterministic verifier is unchanged.

lidar_front_zone._note: front-sector min LaserScan range (geometric, no ML), emitted by the exporter. A state signal ALWAYS emits one evidence: label=obstacle if it crosses inside the search window, else label=clear (a real negative observation). The clear observation is what makes sensor disagreement 'conflicting' rather than 'missing'.

velocity_halt._note: event/crossing signal: emits evidence only when it fires; absence -> required_present false -> low. after:stop_event avoids matching an unrelated early zero.

conclusions.statement: The robot stopped because an obstacle entered the demo safety zone ahead.
conclusions._search_window_note: Hint bracketing the stop hypothesis so anchor_strategy does not scan the whole timeline. The eval harness may override it.

expected._about: The correctness oracle: ONE obstacle-stop spec evaluated against three synthetic datasets must yield three verdicts (matches current eval). Not per-scenario specs.
